findings.description: S04's baseline overlap verification produced false negatives: the comparison method stripped trailing '# <reason>' suffixes from assertion_free_baseline.txt entries, so only bare 'file::test_name' was matched rather than the full line including the suffix. This caused CRITICAL F-01 to flag 11 tests as overlapping when in fact none overlapped the post-CR-00081 merged baseline.
findings.impact: CRITICAL F-01 was raised but was stale — CR-00081 scrubbed the baseline between S04 and S05. S05 corrected the method and verified 0/29 unique tests overlap. No fix cycle was needed.
findings.suggestion: Codify the baseline verification method in the S01 checklist: 'grep -F "<file>::<test_name>" tests/assertion_free_baseline.txt' (exact string including any trailing '# <reason>' suffix). This prevents the suffix-stripping false-negative.

findings.description: S01 report structured fields (labelled_set_size=39, labelled_set_strong_count=21, labelled_set_weak_count=18) are stale — the live labelled_set.jsonl has 40 records (15 STRONG, 0 MEDIUM, 25 WEAK). The file was updated by fix cycles between S01 and S05 submission.
findings.impact: Informational only — does not affect the spike's correctness. The live file is correct and S05 verified it directly.
findings.suggestion: S01 backend reports should include a note: 'Structured fields may be superseded by fix-cycle updates; always verify against live files.' Alternatively, a pre-commit hook could snapshot the actual file stats into the report at generation time.

findings.description: Assertion-scanner violations: (1) test_accepts_well_formed at line 27 has no assertions, (2) test_token_spend_accumulated at line 387 has all-tautology asserts (is not None / isinstance / len > 0). Triggered S12 QV gate failure and one fix cycle.
findings.impact: Fix cycle burned on CR-00084's own code. The fix cycle resolved both violations and S12 passed on re-run. This is a legitimate quality finding in the new unit test suite.
findings.suggestion: Future spike implementations adding new test files should pre-check against the assertion scanner before submitting. Alternatively, the scanner could be run as part of the spike's own pre-flight gates in the agent spec.

findings.description: S11 diff-coverage gate failed with test_e2e_opencode_stub.py::test_session_list_returns_created_sessions — a pre-existing test unrelated to CR-00084. Triggered one fix cycle.
findings.impact: Fix cycle burned on unrelated code; no CR-00084 changes required. S11 passed on re-run.
findings.suggestion: No action needed — this was a pre-existing test issue that the diff-coverage gate surfaced.

findings.description: The per-review cap (< $0.50) is documented in the LIVE form template but is not stated in the DORMANT form. When the hook is re-enabled in a future follow-up CR, the cap must be explicitly stated in the agent-instruction body (not just in the calibration evidence file) to ensure the agent enforces it during live reviews.
findings.impact: No impact now — hook is DORMANT, no live invocation. Will be relevant when a follow-up CR re-enables the hook.
findings.suggestion: When the follow-up CR ships the LIVE form, ensure the per-review cap is declared explicitly in the agent-instruction body. S04's checklist already has this as a gate item — no new process needed.

findings.description: Strategy doc changelog entry dated 2026-05-25 (ship date) vs S03 instruction of 2026-05-24 (design creation date). S04 flagged this as F-02; S05 resolved it as intentional — design date vs ship date are different dates and both are internally consistent within their own documents.
findings.impact: No impact — resolved as intentional. Both dates are used correctly per document convention.
findings.suggestion: No action needed. Document the convention: changelog entries use ship date; tracker rows use design creation date.

findings.description: Evidence file (DEFERRED stub) was initially missing confusion-matrix and metric fields without explanation. S04 F-03 flagged this; S05 resolved it after the file was updated to explicitly document 'CONFUSION MATRIX / METRICS: ABSENT' with a clear reason. The updated evidence file fully satisfies AC3 for a DEFERRED verdict.
findings.impact: No impact — resolved. The evidence file explicitly explains why fields are absent rather than silently omitting them.
findings.suggestion: No action needed. The evidence file template is correct for DEFERRED state.

crITICAL_f-01_resolved_by: S05 re-verification (CR-00081 baseline scrub made finding obsolete; 0/29 unique tests overlap)
test_summary: skipped: no tests for analysis step
notes: Analysis completed; findings written to two output files. Spike-specific focus areas (calibration propagation, cost discipline, advisory-only safety, TDD evidence quality, labelled-set defensibility) all surveyed. CRITICAL F-01 was stale (CR-00081 scrubbed baseline); no fix cycle needed. All 8 QV gates passed (2 with fix cycles, 1 on own code, 1 on unrelated). Advisory-only discipline held throughout. Hook is DORMANT pending re-calibration with valid API key.
